references.title: Confirmation page
references.borrowed: Calm transactional confirmation hierarchy: one clear confirmation panel, next-step wording, and limited component count.
references.rejected: Detached final-success pages as the dominant booking experience; the local blueprint requires same-shell child-route continuity instead.

references.title: Design accessibility guidance
references.borrowed: Explicit heading order, skip-link discipline, and semantic structure guidance for the atlas and patient-route summaries.
references.rejected: Treating section changes as separate-page resets; the local blueprint requires focus-safe same-shell continuity.

references.title: Developing a Keyboard Interface
references.borrowed: Predictable focus movement, visible focus persistence, and composite-widget keyboard conventions for the interactive atlas.
references.rejected: Any focus movement that follows layout convenience instead of logical reading order or preserved active state.

references.title: Landmark Regions
references.borrowed: Named landmark regions and explicit semantic segmentation for atlas canvas, inspector, and lower evidence tables.
references.rejected: Over-fragmenting the atlas into too many unlabeled panels; the value of landmarks drops when regions become excessive.

references.title: Changelog
references.borrowed: Quiet hierarchy, dimmer navigation treatment, and dense-but-stable operational scanning cues.
references.rejected: Issue-tracker-specific chrome or highly branded interaction metaphors that do not fit booking-case contract review.

references.title: Nested Layouts
references.borrowed: Persistent section chrome and child-route continuity as the visual model for booking same-shell route families.
references.rejected: Recomputing layout state on every child-route move; the local blueprint already fixes continuity on route-family contracts.

references.title: New dashboard redesign is now the default
references.borrowed: Consistent tabs and a stable side-rail hierarchy across peer route families.
references.rejected: Floating mobile bottom-bar behavior or collapsible nav patterns that would obscure booking-state inspection.

references.title: Data table usage
references.borrowed: Dense table layout with clear toolbar ownership, scanning-friendly row treatment, and explicit hover/focus distinctions.
references.rejected: Treating evidence tables as generic admin grids with inline mutation affordances; 278 is a contract atlas, not a CRUD board.

references.title: LocatorAssertions
references.borrowed: Locator screenshot assertions as the reference posture for deterministic visual proof.
references.rejected: Using brittle pixel checks without stable locator scope or settled page state.

references.title: Snapshot testing
references.borrowed: ARIA snapshot assertions as the model for machine-readable agreement on semantic structure.
references.rejected: Visual-only proof without accessible-structure parity for the atlas.
